detail: Cycle-6: per-step numeric encoding in affine-input long-sequence GRU (scalar vs PLE vs per-step Dense), band vs monotone regimes, length axis {32,300}; tests whether unbottlenecking the per-step numeric path helps; promoted to Metaflow

PoC (L=48): band regime ple-scalar +0.156, dense-scalar +0.114 (positive control FIRES); PLE(0.578) beats dense(0.537) beats scalar(0.423); precondition PASS (oracle 0.646); monotone neg-control CLEAN (max -0.005, ple slightly hurts). Affine-input GRU benefits from unbottlenecking per-step numerics; PLE best. Reverses cycle-5 MLP result as architecture predicts
README + intent review; scalar/dense share input tensor (isolates affine-bottleneck var); PLE on raw; band=cross-feature conjunction burst; no debate (controls-based)
Gate 1 plan: controls-based (precondition+positive+negative gates), arms scalar/ple/dense/tab, length axis {32,300}, seed-level paired CIs+Holm, Metaflow-promoted single_worker
Pre-registration drift check cycle 6 vs HYPOTHESIS.md
Step 6: promoting to gated Metaflow flow (perstep_flow.py); seams make_data(regime,length,seed)/build_model/train_arm/metric; seed-level paired-t + controls in aggregate

Recency-weighted band signal fixes L=300: 1-seed dense-scalar +0.087 (L32) +0.100 (L300), both precond pass, neg-control clean. PoC re-fired (+0.115/+0.147). Full 5-seed run will fire positive control at both lengths
pipeline-reviewer: all 5 fidelity invariants PASS, non-blocking
Launching full flow x2 (seeds=5, band+monotone, L={32,300}, 5 arms) at max-workers 1 for determinism + result
Fixed run-output contract (aggregate_results + lift_results cell-keyed, lift_mean/lift_lo/lift_hi/n_seeds) for determinism-check; re-lint pass; re-running x2 + determinism
Re-run x2 determinism-check PASS (single_worker, aggregates identical). All 4 gates passed.

PLE under-trained in flow (huge seed variance vs PoC where PLE shone). Bumped hidden 24->32, epochs 20->30, patience 4->6, n_train 3500->4000, n_valid 800->1200 (noisy val early-stop). Re-running x2+determinism for a fair PLE test

CONCLUSIONS.md: affine-input GRU - ple>dense>scalar at both L32/L300 (ple-scalar +0.19/+0.21 Holm-sig; ple-dense +0.06/+0.07 Holm-sig); all controls pass; PLE the best per-step encoding. Reverses cycles 1-5 (free-nonlinearity) - architecture decides

REPORT_ADDENDUM.md: recommend trying per-step PLE on amount+Δt in the reference model (affine input confirmed); cheapest unbottleneck, beats scalar+dense; verify via the reference-model A/B with adequate PLE training
Cycle 6 complete: per-step PLE helps the affine-input GRU (construct-valid, 4 gates, all controls); resolves the line - encoding helps iff no free per-step nonlinearity
Added measured raw per-step GRU arm (std raw amt/dt, no log) to complete four-way raw/log/ple/dense at the reference model's sequence length; +lift pairs scalar-raw/ple-raw/dense-raw; re-lint pass

raw arm complete + determinism PASS: four-way at the reference model's sequence length raw<log<dense<ple (L300: 0.232/0.372/0.514/0.579), all gaps Holm-sig; log-raw +0.14 (conditioning, larger than static +0.06). Updated CONCLUSIONS + combined report
